commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds API-key lookup to cron local-provider preflight and sends an Authorization bearer header on the GET /models or /api/tags probe when a key is found.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main sends the local provider preflight GET without headers, and the PR body includes LiteLLM before/after proxy logs for the symptom. I did not run a live LiteLLM/vLLM instance in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This is a safe repair candidate because the blockers are confined to cron preflight credential resolution and focused regression coverage, with no new config or product decision required.

target.title: fix(cron): add Authorization header to provider preflight probe
target.body:
## Summary

The cron job preflight check sends `GET {baseUrl}/models` to verify local provider connectivity before running a job, but was doing so **without an Authorization header**. For providers behind auth middleware (LiteLLM, vLLM, etc.), this generates spurious 401 errors on every cron run.

## Root Cause

`model-preflight.runtime.ts` → `probeLocalProviderEndpoint()` called `fetchWithSsrFGuard` with `init: { method: "GET" }` — no auth header.

## Fix

Added Authorization header from the provider's API key, resolved from the same sources as normal inference calls (config apiKey → auth-profiles.json → env var).

## Real behavior proof

### Before (LiteLLM proxy log, 2026-05-18)
```
INFO: 127.0.0.1:54237 - "GET /models HTTP/1.1" 401 Unauthorized
INFO: 127.0.0.1:54452 - "POST /chat/completions HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
```

### After (LiteLLM proxy log, 2026-05-18)
```
INFO: 127.0.0.1:57749 - "GET /models HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
INFO: 127.0.0.1:57751 - "POST /chat/completions HTTP/1.1" 200 OK
```

Both requests now carry the same Authorization header, confirmed by sequential source ports (57749 → 57751) from the same process. Tested on a live OpenClaw 2026.5.12 gateway with LiteLLM proxy.

## Changes

`src/cron/isolated-agent/model-preflight.runtime.ts`:
- Added `resolveProbeApiKey()` that resolves API key from config apiKey, auth-profiles.json, or env var
- `probeLocalProviderEndpoint` now receives and uses the resolved API key
- If no key is found (authless servers like Ollama), probe proceeds without auth — same as before
